docs/next/lit/RAG.md:417:| **P3-D-RAGC** | Spec the matched generic-RAG/tool-use control: same pinned source snapshot, matched byte/compute budgets, factorial cells per §2.2(2)f — **the executable matched control itself is still to be designed** | **Supplies:** pinned-source-snapshot + provenance-scoring pattern (KILT — scope-limited: no same-retriever or cross-representation precedent, §6.1); BM25 + dense cells mandatory; retrieval-recall-vs-answer separation; position-shuffle, random-document, long-tail-stratified cells; pinned standard harness; identical index-bytes + build-cost + query-cost ledger on both arms (§3, §6). **OPEN — must be designed with no published precedent (§6 rules 1–5, §7.1):** equivalent source-content derivation + information-parity criterion (kernel records vs passages vs triples vs tool schemas index *different objects*); identical-vs-architecture-specific retriever policy; retrieved-token/context budgets + ordering parity; generator/tool-executor/retry-policy/tuning-compute parity; construction-leakage + authoring-effort accounting. **Not closed** — closest of the three, but "same source snapshot" is not yet "matched control". |
docs/next/lit/RAG.md:418:| **P3-D-FRONTIER** | The neural frontier-builder: per-budget strongest baselines — quantisation/pruning/distillation + retrieval per §1 recipes + **difficulty-adaptive test-time compute (Snell-style, NOT best-of-k)** + learned verifiers (outcome + auto-labelled PRM) | **Supplies:** ICLR-2025 citation verified with envelope (>4× vs best-of-N and 14×-larger-model parity are *separate, conditional* findings — PaLM-2/MATH/fine-tuned revisers+PRMs, not our band, not one exchange rate; §4); large-budget verifier decay must be reported (scaling-flaws — shown for the tested verifiers, not universal); verifier recipe *direction* (Cobbe outcome + Math-Shepherd auto-PRM, no human-label budget); ≤2B tool-use recipe direction (APIGen breadth / TinyAgent — whose DeBERTa selector goes on the ledger) (§1, §4, §5). **OPEN — "implement Snell-style" is not yet a baseline recipe:** budgeted difficulty estimator (compute charged); eligible search policies per budget rung; verifier training/build costs on the ledger; fallback where estimated base success ≈ 0; non-math calibration gate. |
docs/next/lit/RAG.md:419:| **P3-D-GNN (co-input)** | H-GNN designs + the size×depth sweep + six controls (P3-LR-FUSE leads; this review co-feeds the retrieval side) | **Supplies:** vanilla RAG frequently beats GraphRAG on flat tasks (GraphRAG-Bench) — the text-serialisation control is a *live threat*, not a formality; no published size×depth sweep exists, so the §3.2 sweep is novel; charge graph construction to the ledger explicitly (§2, §3). **OPEN — this review does NOT de-risk the GNN bet:** every graph source here is retrieval/indexing, **no GNN-to-LM-fusion paper is in this ledger**; "structure wins past a hops/aggregation threshold" and "helps more as the generator shrinks" are *hypotheses* (MiniRAG's causal reading is the authors' interpretation, §2) — the empirical premise stays with P3-LR-FUSE, and the design bead must carry an explicit "no prior" statement. |

   204	### 2.4 The anti-weak-control channel (threat model, this design's mirror of weak-builder gaming)
   205	
   206	The party building S also derives S's control — an under-built generic arm
   207	manufactures fake kernel-semantics attribution, exactly as an under-searched
   208	frontier manufactures a fake W1 (FRONT/1 §7.3's channel, mirrored on the control
   209	side). Counters [STIPULATED: ASM-0922, extending the ASM-0812 threat model]:
   210	
   211	1. GR-A/GR-B are built and tuned by the FRONT/1 pipeline under the SAME T_k
   212	   meter and fixed selection rule as every comparator — the control's strength
   213	   is bought with the same metered budget as S's tuning, never less.
   214	2. The pinned standard harness (§3.3) supplies the community's default recipes;
   215	   deviating BELOW harness defaults requires a logged reason.
   216	3. FRONT/1's nomination window + F-CAL A4 challenger gate apply to the GR arms
   217	   as F(B_k) members — outsiders can strengthen the control we would be tempted
   218	   to weaken.
   219	4. Full derivation + build logs published (no-silent-caps applied to the
   220	   control's construction).

   224	## 3. Rule 2 executed — the retriever decision table
   225	
   226	FRONT/1 §5 Rule 2 (SHARED/MATCHED cell required in F(B_k) + W1 control set;
   227	NATIVE cell additional; disclosed handicap, never exclusion) is inherited
   228	verbatim [STIPULATED: ASM-0853 inherited]. RAGC pins the mechanics:
   229	
   230	### 3.1 The shared retriever, and what "same retriever over different objects" means
   231	
   232	- DECISION: the shared cell's retriever pair is **BM25 + one pinned small dense
   233	  embedder** (name+revision hashed at freeze; embedder weights + index bytes
   234	  charged to the arm's store_bytes cap), run over a **pinned common text
   235	  serialisation** of every arm's objects: kernel records via the existing
   236	  deterministic renderer/desugaring path; triples as canonical
   237	  subject–predicate–object sentences; tool schemas as their docstrings; passages
   238	  as themselves. The serialiser is deterministic, versioned, content-hashed —
   239	  the shared cell compares CONTENT REPRESENTATIONS under one fixed retrieval
   240	  lens, which is exactly its claim licence [STIPULATED: ASM-0922]. BM25 is
   241	  mandatory in every instantiation — a structured-store win that never ran the
   242	  lexical baseline is unattributed [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.2 (BEIR,
   243	  arXiv:2104.08663, NeurIPS 2021)].
   244	- DECISION: the per-cell identical-vs-native decision (the MF0 §8.2(iii)
   245	  delegation) is closed as a fixed table, not per-experiment judgement
   246	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0922]:
   247	
   248	| Comparison being made | Retriever policy |
   249	|---|---|
   250	| attribution: representation at fixed retrieval (GR-A vs S-serialised) | SHARED (BM25 + pinned dense), both arms |
   251	| frontier: strongest conventional system (GR-B) | NATIVE — builder's best in-budget choice, tuned within T_k |
   252	| executor axis (GR-C vs S) | SHARED addressing where S's addressing is retrieval-like; else the tool-call interface IS the addressing and is measured as such, disclosed |
   253	| retry axis (GR-D vs S) | inherit whichever cell GR-D wraps |
   254	
   255	- Disclosure rule: where the shared lens structurally handicaps an arm whose
   256	  native retrieval operates over different objects (kernel addressing most of
   257	  all), the handicap is stated beside the result and the NATIVE cell carries the
   258	  strongest-system reading — never grounds to drop the shared cell (FRONT/1
   259	  Rule 2 corrected form, inherited) [STIPULATED: ASM-0853 inherited].
   260	
   261	### 3.2 Retrieval metrics, separated from end-task metrics
   262	
   263	- DECISION: every arm reports retrieval recall@k against gold provenance (where
   264	  the P5 annotation supplies gold spans) SEPARATELY from end-task accuracy —
   265	  retrieval quality and generation quality move independently and attribution
   266	  requires both readouts [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.3 (FiD, arXiv:2007.01282,
   267	  EACL 2021); STIPULATED: ASM-0922].
   268	
   269	### 3.3 The harness pin
   270	
   271	- DECISION: implementation from one pinned standard harness (FlashRAG-class),
   272	  **release pinned at freeze** — the v1/v2 method-count discrepancy is the
   273	  standing lesson that un-pinned harness versions corrupt comparability
   274	  [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §3, FlashRAG v2 abstract re-fetched 2026-07-11;
   275	  STIPULATED: ASM-0922].
   276	
   277	---

   279	## 4. Rule 3 executed — retrieved-token/context budgets + ordering
   280	
   281	FRONT/1 §5 Rule 3 (pinned max retrieved tokens per query identical across arms;
   282	max context length; score-descending assembly; no hand-placement at context
   283	edges) is inherited verbatim [STIPULATED: ASM-0853 inherited]. RAGC pins:
   284	
   285	- DECISION: per rung, ONE retrieved-token budget `ret_tok(B_k)` and ONE context
   286	  cap for every arm including S; k (objects retrieved) floats per arm to fill
   287	  the SAME token budget — matching tokens, not object counts, because objects
   288	  differ in size across representations by construction [STIPULATED: ASM-0923].
   289	- DECISION: assembly order = retrieval-score-descending for every arm; the PS
   290	  cell (§1.1) is the pre-registered order perturbation; its seed is pinned
   291	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0923; the position confound is measured art, LIT-BACKED:
   292	  RAG.md §6.4].
   293	- DECISION: the RD cell fills the same token budget with seed-pinned random
   294	  snapshot documents; RD is read as a control ONLY (if GR-A − RD ≈ 0, the
   295	  "retrieval win" is a prompt-perturbation effect and the retrieval attribution
   296	  is void at that scope); tuning toward RD-style noise injection is prohibited
   297	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0923; LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §7.7].
   298	- DECISION: cross-reference for token-parity with the content-type axis: the
   299	  kernel-as-text null (cell (g), owner MF0 §8.5) runs at the SAME `ret_tok(B_k)`
   300	  budget, so the null, the generic control, and S are token-commensurable in
   301	  one experiment [STIPULATED: ASM-0923].

   305	## 5. Rule 4 executed — generator / executor / retry / tuning parity
   306	
   307	FRONT/1 §5 Rule 4 (identical generator checkpoint, decoding, retry budget,
   308	abstention accounting, executor semantics for store-vs-store comparisons;
   309	free-corpus/whole-system arms exempt with the narrower licence) is inherited
   310	verbatim [STIPULATED: ASM-0853 inherited]. RAGC makes the retry and executor
   311	sides concrete — they are where the f2b confound lives.
   312	
   313	### 5.1 Generator parity
   314	
   315	- DECISION: for every attribution comparison (GR-A/GR-C/GR-D/R0/perturbation
   316	  cells vs S): same generator checkpoint (hash), same decoding parameters, same
   317	  max-token + stop discipline, same abstention token semantics, same prompt
   318	  template SKELETON with only the evidence slot varying (template pinned,
   319	  per-arm evidence rendering pinned by §3.1's serialisers)
   320	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0924].
   321	
   322	### 5.2 Retry-policy parity (GR-D — the f2b de-confound generalised)
   323	
   324	The measured sign the programme owns is a verify-RETRY loop; its assessment says
   325	the shuffled control cannot separate content from alignment [MEASURED:
   326	registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json]. The generic control must therefore
   327	match the LOOP, not just the store:
   328	
   329	- DECISION: GR-D wraps a generic arm in S's exact loop shape — same max-k
   330	  retries, same per-retry prompt-mutation rule, same abstain-on-exhaustion —
   331	  with the acceptance signal drawn from a pinned generic menu, chosen per
   332	  consuming experiment and named in its prereg [STIPULATED: ASM-0924]:
   333	  (i) self-consistency agreement at a pinned threshold (SA-class components
   334	  only, per FRONT/1 §4.2 semantics); (ii) a small learned outcome verifier
   335	  trained within T_k and fitting store_bytes (Cobbe-direction, auto-labels
   336	  only [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §5 (Cobbe arXiv:2110.14168, 2021; Math-Shepherd
   337	  ACL 2024 auto-label recipe)]); (iii) tool-execution success where the
   338	  vertical has an executable surface (GR-C's executor as the checker).
   339	- DECISION: the readout is the pair (S − GR-D) and (GR-D − GR-A): the first
   340	  isolates what the KERNEL verifier adds over a generic acceptance signal in
   341	  the same loop; the second prices the loop shape itself (retry/search axis of
   342	  the six-way factorisation) [STIPULATED: ASM-0924].
   343	
   344	### 5.3 Executor parity (GR-C — the generic-executor cell, frozen)
   345	
   346	- DECISION: the MF0 §8.5 executor-axis draft cells are frozen here as:
   347	  **engine-off** (S's store retrieved, execution disabled — owned by the
   348	  consuming experiment, listed for completeness) and **GR-C generic-executor
   349	  substitution**: the same typed content (triples row of D) loaded into one
   350	  pinned generic deterministic engine (proposed default: SQLite with a pinned
   351	  recursive-CTE query library; a pinned open Datalog engine is the registered
   352	  alternative), exposed to the generator through the tool schemas row of D;
   353	  tool-call syntax errors, timeouts, and refusals accounted identically to S's
   354	  engine errors [STIPULATED: ASM-0924; absorbs round-1 A.1's mandatory
   355	  generic-Datalog attribution cell]. If S ≈ GR-C on the covered slice, the
   356	  kernel ENGINE's distinctive contribution (beyond generic deterministic
   357	  execution over the same typed content) is dead at that scope — this is
   358	  K-P3v2(4)'s attribution-collapse input at the executor axis
   359	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0924].
   360	
   361	### 5.4 Tool-executor semantics + the oracle cell
   362	
   363	- DECISION: sandbox, timeout, retry-on-tool-error, and error-message surface are
   364	  pinned identically for every executor-bearing arm (S's engine, GR-C, any
   365	  tool-use comparator); the OR oracle-retrieval cell is co-frozen with
   366	  P3-D-ORACLE so the G2→G3 decomposition and this control use one instrument,
   367	  not two [STIPULATED: ASM-0924].
   368	
   369	### 5.5 Tuning parity
   370	
   371	- DECISION: tuning-compute parity is NOT re-specified here — it binds through
   372	  FRONT/1 §3.3's T_k resource-vector meter and arm table (the GR arms are arm
   373	  keys C3-RAG-matched / C3-RAG-free already); GR-C and GR-D each get ONE arm-key
   374	  addition to the arm table at rung entry (a frontier version-bump event if
   375	  added later, per FRONT/1's closed-arm-table rule) [STIPULATED: ASM-0852
   376	  consumed; ASM-0924].

   380	## 6. Rule 5 executed — construction leakage + authoring effort
   381	
   382	FRONT/1 §5 Rule 5 (every construction pass booked to KOT-LIFE/1 + KOT-SIZE/2 on
   383	every arm identically; derivation pipelines frozen before any frozen-suite item
   384	is drawn; screen applies to construction inputs and outputs; post-exposure
   385	construction VOID for W1) is inherited verbatim [STIPULATED: ASM-0853 inherited].
   386	RAGC adds the per-arm ledger table as the executable artifact:
   387	
   388	- DECISION: every instantiation publishes one **build-ledger row per arm**:
   389	  {derivation script hash, derivation CPU/GPU compute, human hours, index build
   390	  compute, embedder training/selection compute (TinyAgent-DeBERTa-class
   391	  selectors included [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1 (TinyAgent, arXiv:2409.00608,
   392	  EMNLP 2024)]), packed object bytes, index bytes,
   393	  screen result hash, freeze timestamp vs suite-exposure timestamp} — the
   394	  literature's silent asymmetry (dense index ≈ free embed pass; graph/store
   395	  build ≈ full sweep) becomes an explicit line item on BOTH sides
   396	  [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §3 (accounting-practice table, sources 2020–2025);
   397	  STIPULATED: ASM-0925].
   398	- DECISION: kernel-store authoring hours (explicator work) appear on S's row of
   399	  the SAME table — the control's fairness cuts both ways; no arm's construction
   400	  is free [STIPULATED: ASM-0925].
   401	
   402	---
   403	
   404	## 7. The readout: six-way attribution, operationalised
   405	
   406	The six-way factorisation (kernel semantics / structured storage / retrieval /
   407	deterministic execution / retry-search / integration; ASM-0812) is decomposed as
   408	far as the instantiated cells allow (MF0 §8.5 rule 2). RAGC pins the contrast
   409	ladder and the licensed sentences [STIPULATED: ASM-0926]:
   410	
   411	| Contrast | Isolates (as far as cells allow) | If ≈ 0 |
   412	|---|---|---|
   413	| S − cell (g) kernel-as-text | store machinery vs plain conditioning | machinery adds nothing over text at matched tokens |
   414	| S − cell (e) aligned-non-kernel | kernel SEMANTICS vs typed store (our own instrument; no published precedent — carries the load alone [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6 closing]) | "NSM semantics" attribution dead; "typed store + checker" reading stands |
   415	| S − GR-A | kernel system vs conventional retrieval at fixed content + retrieval lens | the win is available to any RAG baseline → K-P3v2(4) input |
   416	| GR-A − R0 | retrieval contribution on this vertical | the evidence isn't doing anything retrievable |
   417	| GR-A − RD | retrieval RELEVANCE vs prompt perturbation | "retrieval win" was noise-shaped |
   418	| S − GR-C | kernel engine vs generic deterministic executor, same typed content | engine's distinctive claim dead at scope (§5.3) |
   419	| S − GR-D | kernel verifier content vs generic acceptance signal in the same loop | the f2b-shape win is loop-generic |
   420	| GR-D − GR-A | the retry/search axis itself | the loop shape isn't the lever |
   421	
   422	- DECISION: reporting requirements on every instantiation: (1) the full contrast
   423	  vector for the named cells, never the headline alone; (2) covered-vs-uncovered
   424	  split wherever a store is in play (ASM-0811's standing vector rule);
   425	  (3) long-tail/popularity-stratified readout where the vertical admits a
   426	  popularity proxy — unstratified averages hide whether the store or parametric
   427	  memory answered [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.6 (Mallen, arXiv:2212.10511,
   428	  ACL 2023)]; (4) retrieval recall@k
   429	  reported beside every end-task figure (§3.2); (5) each sentence in the verdict
   430	  names the cell that licenses it (FRONT/1 Rule 2's licence discipline,
   431	  generalised) [STIPULATED: ASM-0926].
   432	- DECISION: K-P3v2(4) wiring, verbatim: "every measured family gain vanishes
   433	  against the matched generic-store / generic-RAG controls" is evaluated on the
   434	  S − GR-A and S − GR-C contrasts under the §2.5 house statistics
   435	  (LCB-vs-margin, FWER across the instantiated contrast set, hierarchical
   436	  bootstrap preserving paired predictions) — RAGC supplies the cells; the
   437	  analysis plan supplies the test [STIPULATED: ASM-0926, consuming ASM-0813].
   438	
   439	---
   440	
   441	## 8. The executable interface: what a consuming experiment and FRONT/1 receive
   442	
   443	### 8.1 The control-arm manifest (the plug-in object)
   444	
   445	- DECISION: RAGC's implementation (P3-T-RAGC-IMPL, §11) emits per
   446	  vertical × rung one content-hashed **control-arm manifest**:
   447	  `{snapshot_hash, derivation_matrix: D rows with script hashes, object hashes
   448	  and byte counts, serialiser hashes, retriever pins (BM25 config, dense
   449	  embedder name+revision), index hashes, harness release pin, ret_tok(B_k),
   450	  context cap, ordering rule with PS/RD seeds, generator pin and decoding
   451	  params, GR-C executor pin and tool-schema hash, GR-D loop params and
   452	  acceptance-signal choice, build-ledger rows, parity diagnostics (κ vector,
   453	  a vector, ε_par), screen results}` [STIPULATED: ASM-0927].
   454	- DECISION: consumption contract [STIPULATED: ASM-0927]:
   455	  - **FRONT/1** consumes the manifest at S0 (GR-A/GR-B enter the candidate
   456	    lattice as the C3-matched cells with their derivations already pinned) and
   457	    measures/pins them through S1–S6 like any comparator — RAGC never runs its
   458	    own frontier measurement;
   459	  - **every store-touching [EXP]** consumes the manifest at prereg freeze: the
   460	    prereg names instantiated arms/cells, discrimination claims, ε_par, and the
   461	    contrast set; a store-touching G4 prereg that lacks the manifest hash does
   462	    not freeze (fail-closed);
   463	  - amendments to any inherited FRONT/1 §5 rule go through a registered
   464	    amendment + manifest version bump — never silently (ASM-0853's clause).
   465	
   466	### 8.2 Per-experiment instantiation checklist (what the prereg must name)
   467	
   468	1. Vertical + snapshot hash; 2. arms + cells instantiated, each with its
   469	discrimination claim; 3. budgets (store_bytes, ret_tok, context cap, retry k);
   470	4. retriever cells (GR-A.bm25 mandatory; GR-A.dense; GR-B config); 5. GR-D
   471	acceptance signal; 6. ε_par + the parity-diagnostic sample size from P3-D-POWER;
   472	7. the contrast set + its FWER family; 8. which contrasts feed K-P3v2(4)
   473	[STIPULATED: ASM-0927].
   474	
   475	### 8.3 Worked example (design-level; the first instantiation target)
   476	
   477	The shape inherited from round-1 (the W1 hypergraph's source-parity two-arm
   478	design, salvaged to this bead) applied to the programme's two existing verticals
   479	[STIPULATED: ASM-0927]:
   480	
   481	- Snapshot: the a5 vertical's pinned source repository (code + docs), the same
   482	  material the measured world layer was deterministically extracted from
   483	  [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/a5.json 855/855 on that substrate].
   484	- D rows: kernel records = the existing a5 world layer (already extant, hash
   485	  pinned); passages = chunked source/docs; triples = the deterministic code
   486	  extractor's output re-serialised; tool schemas = lookup/check functions over
   487	  the triple store in SQLite.
   488	- Arms: GR-A/GR-C/GR-D/R0 + PS/RD/OR cells; generator = the f2b 135M host;
   489	  GR-D acceptance = tool-execution success (the vertical is executable).
   490	- What it buys: the missing generic-RAG/generic-executor/generic-retry arms for
   491	  the ONLY measured end-task sign — the f2b de-confound the assessment says is
   492	  required before the +0.1507 licenses anything [MEASURED:
   493	  registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json]. This instantiation is the natural
   494	  first consumer bead (P3-E-VL-1's comparator set names these cells already,
   495	  programme §3.1).
   496	
   497	---
   498	
   499	## 9. Validation gates (RAGC-CAL) — the control is an instrument and gets instrument gates
   500	
   501	Pre-registered acceptance tests, co-scheduled with P3-E-FCAL/P3-E-CAL (same
   502	free-pool calibration campaign) [STIPULATED: ASM-0927]:
   503	
   504	- **V1 — harness reproduction (plumbing):** the pinned harness release
   505	  reproduces one published method×dataset figure from its own documentation
   506	  within a pre-registered tolerance. Catches tooling breakage, not weakness.
   507	- **V2 — discrimination sanity (per vertical, LCB-based):** on the dev split,
   508	  OR ≥ GR-A ≥ R0 in the expected order with LCB95(OR − R0) > 0 on a
   509	  knowledge-intensive dev slice. If OR ≈ R0, the vertical's dev slice cannot
   510	  discriminate evidence use AT ALL and every RAGC contrast on it is
   511	  uninformative — the instantiation is blocked there, disclosed, not fudged.
   512	- **V3 — build repeatability:** two independent derivation+index builds
   513	  (same seeds) produce byte-identical BM25 indexes and dense indexes within a
   514	  pre-registered band; the manifest is re-derivable from pins alone.
   515	- **V4 — parity diagnostics in band:** §2.3's κ/a vectors computed; ε_par test
   516	  applied per §2.3's downgrade rule.
   517	- **Fail-closed rule:** no W1-relevant [EXP] cites any RAGC cell until V1–V3
   518	  PASS for its vertical×rung manifest; V4 governs the attribution licence per
   519	  §2.3. Mirrors FRONT/1 §8's builder fail-closed discipline
   520	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0927].
   521	
   522	Falsifiability of the design as a whole: RAGC/1 is falsified as an instrument by
   523	its own gates (V2 can fail on a real vertical; V3 on a real toolchain; V4 on a
   524	real derivation), and its central stipulation — that a token/byte/compute-matched
   525	generic control is CONSTRUCTIBLE on the programme's verticals inside the free
   526	pool (§10) — is falsified if the §10 envelope is exhausted before V1–V3 pass
   527	[STIPULATED: ASM-0927].
   528	
   529	---
   530	
   531	## 10. Compute plan (cheap-first; worked arithmetic)
   532	
   533	Planning estimates, not commitments; final at KOT-FAIR/2 freeze; local-box work
   534	`nice -n 10` + checkpointed per standing practice [STIPULATED: ASM-0927]:
   535	
   536	- **Derivations + indexes (per vertical, R-1):** chunking/triple/tool-schema
   537	  derivation = CPU-only on snapshots ≤ ~100 MB; BM25 index = CPU; dense embed
   538	  pass with a small pinned embedder ≈ ≤ ~1 GPU-h. SQLite/Datalog GR-C store
   539	  build = CPU.
   540	- **Parity diagnostics:** P5 is annotation work (power-sized, blind-sampled —
   541	  costed as annotation, not ~$0, per the round-1 correction to census
   542	  accounting); P6 rides the OR cell ≈ ≤ ~1 GPU-h per vertical.
   543	- **GR-A/GR-B frontier measurement:** inside FRONT/1's §10 ledger (the C3 arm
   544	  keys) — NOT double-counted here.
   545	- **Perturbation cells (PS/RD/OR) + GR-C/GR-D at dev scale:** ~3–5 configs ×
   546	  ~1,500 dev items × ≤ ~1,024 tokens ≈ ≤ ~8M tokens ≈ **≤ ~3 GPU-h per
   547	  vertical×rung** at 135M-class throughput.
   548	- **RAGC total beyond FRONT's ledger, R-1, both verticals: ≤ ~10 GPU-h** + CPU +
   549	  one costed annotation batch — inside the ARC/Modal free pool. R-2 scales
   550	  ≈ 3–4×; R-3 rides the same secured-allocation condition as FRONT/1 §10
   551	  (fail-closed if no allocation).
   552	- Graceful degradation: BM25-only GR-A + GR-C + RD/PS cells are CPU-feasible end
   553	  to end at 135M-class with a single free GPU for generation — a valid (weaker,
   554	  disclosed) control version can still freeze if the dense embedder or GR-D
   555	  verifier training is unavailable [STIPULATED: ASM-0927].
   556	
   557	---
   558	
   559	## 11. Beads this design spawns (recommendation — the coordinator creates them; no bd operation is performed by this document)
   560	
   561	```
   562	P3-T-RAGC-IMPL  [task, P1]  Implement the control-arm pipeline: derivation
   563	    matrix D (chunker/triple/tool-schema scripts + serialisers), BM25+dense
   564	    shared index build, GR-C SQLite/Datalog executor + tool schemas, GR-D loop
   565	    wrapper + generic acceptance signals, PS/RD/OR cells, manifest emitter
   566	    (§8.1), build-ledger rows, parity-diagnostic tooling (P5 containment
   567	    checker, P6 probe harness). Pinned-harness (FlashRAG-class) based; keep
   568	    OUT of encoder/; CPU/single-GPU first. Blocked-by: P3-T-FRONT-IMPL
   569	    (shares the packing script, T_k meter, and arm-table ledger).
   570	P3-E-RAGC-CAL  [task, P1]  Pre-register + run V1–V4 per vertical×rung
   571	    manifest; co-scheduled with P3-E-FCAL/P3-E-CAL. Fail-closed per §9.
   572	Dependency edges for the coordinator: every store-touching G4 [EXP] adds
   573	    blocked-by {P3-T-RAGC-IMPL, P3-E-RAGC-CAL PASS for its vertical}; the
   574	    P3-E-VL-1 prereg names the §8.3 worked-example manifest.
   575	```
   576	
   577	---
   578	
   579	## 12. Honest limits of this design (carried on every consuming claim)
   580	
   581	1. **Information parity is measured, not certified** — the four inherited checks
   582	   + the P5/P6 diagnostics + the ε_par downgrade bound the confound; they do not
   583	   eliminate it. The residual limitation line rides every attribution sentence
   584	   (FRONT/1 Rule 1, inherited).
   585	2. **The shared retrieval lens is itself a choice** — BM25 + one dense embedder
   586	   over pinned serialisations is A fixed lens, not THE neutral lens; the NATIVE
   587	   cell and the disclosure rule are the mitigation, not a solution.
   588	3. **Generic acceptance signals are not exhaustive** — GR-D's menu (agreement /
   589	   small learned verifier / execution success) covers the published recipe
   590	   space at our budgets [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §5, sources 2021–2024], but "no
   591	   generic signal matches
   592	   the kernel verifier" licenses only "none of the MENU matches", never a
   593	   universal.
   594	4. **The control cannot rescue an unpowered comparison** — every contrast's
   595	   sample size comes from P3-D-POWER under the realised FWER family; RAGC
   596	   supplies cells, never power.
   597	5. **V2 can render a vertical uninformative** — that is a property of the
   598	   vertical/dev slice, disclosed and blocking, not repairable inside RAGC.
   599	6. **ε_par, chunker defaults, executor default (SQLite), and the GPU-hour
   600	   envelope are stipulated planning values**, maintainer-adjustable at KOT-FAIR/2
   601	   freeze; the §10 arithmetic re-derives from measured P3-E-CAL throughput.
   602	
   603	---
   604	
   605	## 13. Registered assumption entries
   606	
   607	Fresh append-only block ASM-0920–0939 (this bead); entries 0928–0939 remain free.
   608	
   609	| Registered id | Scope |
   610	|---|---|
   611	| **ASM-0920** | RAGC contract: the closed named arm inventory GR-A/GR-B/GR-C/GR-D/R0 + PS/RD/OR cells with per-arm claim licences; gate placement (G1 diagnostics, G2 oracle cells, G4 mandatory); NL-integrity applied to the control (never NLB-gated); rung-anchor generators (§0–§1) |
   612	| **ASM-0921** | Rule 1 executed: the derivation matrix D (pinned chunker/deterministic-triple/tool-schema rows), byte-cap mechanics, and the information-parity instrument — P5 decisive-span coverage, P6 oracle-delivery probe, ε_par band with fail-closed attribution downgrade (§2.1–2.3) |
   613	| **ASM-0922** | Rule 2 executed: shared-retriever mechanics (BM25 + pinned dense over pinned common serialisations), the closed identical-vs-native decision table, recall-vs-answer separation, harness release pin, and the anti-weak-control counters (§2.4, §3) |
   614	| **ASM-0923** | Rule 3 executed: one retrieved-token budget per rung with floating k, score-descending assembly, PS/RD cell semantics (RD control-only), token-commensurability with the kernel-as-text null (§4) |
   615	| **ASM-0924** | Rule 4 executed: generator parity mechanics; GR-D retry-parity with the pinned generic acceptance-signal menu and its two-contrast readout; GR-C generic-executor cell frozen (SQLite/Datalog default, absorbing the generic-Datalog control); executor-semantics parity; arm-table keys for GR-C/GR-D (§5) |
   616	| **ASM-0925** | Rule 5 executed: the per-arm build-ledger row schema; kernel authoring hours on S's row of the same table (§6) |
   617	| **ASM-0926** | The readout: the contrast ladder operationalising the six-way attribution factorisation, the five reporting requirements (contrast vector, covered split, long-tail strata, recall separation, sentence-to-cell licensing), and the K-P3v2(4) wiring through §2.5 statistics (§7) |
   618	| **ASM-0927** | The executable interface + instrument gates: the control-arm manifest schema and consumption contract (FRONT S0; prereg fail-closed), instantiation checklist, worked example on the a5/f2b vertical, RAGC-CAL V1–V4 with fail-closed rule, compute envelope + graceful degradation (§8–§10) |
   619	
   620	---
   621	
   622	## Epistemic register (what this design relied on)
   623	
   624	- **STIPULATED (registered block ASM-0920…ASM-0927):** every design choice above;
   625	  each is a scoping/mechanism decision, none is evidence for any architecture
   626	  family or for either value thesis. Inherited binding stipulations consumed:
   627	  ASM-0853 (FRONT/1 §5 rules, verbatim), ASM-0852 (T_k meter/arm table),
   628	  ASM-0890/0891 (MF0 §8.2(iii)/§8.5 ownership), ASM-0812/0813/0814/0808/0817
   629	  (controls, statistics, oracle labelling, NL integrity, gates).
   630	- **MEASURED (restated strictly inside their envelopes):** the zero-audited-wins
   631	  null (feasibility-synthesis §0); the f2b +0.1507 with its does_not_license
   632	  assessment (the confound this control exists to cut); the NL-boundary FAILs
   633	  l3a-parse 47.6% / a5-nl 41.6% + S2; the a5 855/855 deterministic world-layer
   634	  substrate used in the worked example (registry/verdicts + assessments as cited
   635	  inline).
   636	- **LIT-BACKED (through the completed P3-LR-RAG review, sources verified at
   637	  primary venue 2026-07-11):** the five open rules + "cells, not the control"
   638	  verdict (RAG.md §6/§7.1); BM25-mandatory (BEIR); recall-vs-answer separation
   639	  (FiD); position confound (Lost-in-the-Middle); random-document trap
   640	  (Power-of-Noise, control-only); long-tail stratification (Mallen); harness
   641	  variance + release pinning (FlashRAG); construction-cost asymmetry (RAG.md §3);
   642	  selector-on-the-ledger (TinyAgent); the verifier recipe menu (RAG.md §5).
   643	- **EXTRAPOLATION:** none used as a premise. The only forward-looking values
   644	  (ε_par default, chunker defaults, GPU-hour envelope) are stipulated planning
   645	  numbers listed in §12 as maintainer-adjustable, not projections about any
   646	  system's behaviour.
   647	
   648	This document changes no frozen object, no verdict, no audit; its eight
   649	assumption entries are REGISTERED in registry/assumptions.jsonl (append-only
   650	block ASM-0920–0927) by this design. No git, bd, or kb-sync operation is
   651	performed.

item.text: A few load-bearing issues are emerging: the arm inventory is implementable, but several stated contrasts are not actually single-factor causal contrasts, and the proposed validation gate can reject a perfectly valid RAG control. I’m checking those against the exact FAIR/resource contract before ranking them.

   315	### 1.3 Inference cost — KOT-COST/2 (the resource vector) + the neural-FLOP diagnostic
   316	
   317	The review's technical error #7 is accepted: a ledger in which symbolic
   318	computation enters at zero is not a compute measure — a system doing substantial
   319	CPU search, retrieval, decompression, or I/O could look "compute-matched" by
   320	definition. Revision 1's L1 is therefore RENAMED and DEMOTED to what it actually
   321	is, and the binding ledger becomes a full resource vector [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   322	
   323	- DECISION: inference cost is the measured **resource vector KOT-COST/2**, per
   324	  query and per suite, on pinned hardware under the P3-D-HW repeatability
   325	  protocol: (a) accelerator time + estimated accelerator ops; (b) CPU-seconds;
   326	  (c) bytes read from storage and network; (d) energy (RAPL on CPU,
   327	  NVML/`nvidia-smi`-class counters on GPU, whole-run integration — not query-level
   328	  spot reads); (e) peak accelerator memory AND host RSS; (f) end-to-end latency at
   329	  p50 and p95; (g) throughput under a pinned concurrency distribution; (h) warm-
   330	  and cold-cache conditions both measured, including startup/index-load time;
   331	  (i) time-to-first-token and inter-token latency for generative arms; (j) output
   332	  length controlled (pinned max-token + stop discipline) or cost normalised per
   333	  emitted token, pinned per benchmark. Methodology borrows MLPerf inference +
   334	  power measurement discipline (REVIEW-CITED: MLPerf inference-datacenter
   335	  benchmarks + MLPerf power methodology; verified and operationalised by
   336	  P3-LR-SYS → P3-D-HW before the framework freezes) [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   337	- DECISION: the analytic neural-FLOP ledger survives as the **neural-FLOP
   338	  diagnostic** (2·params·tokens + attention terms, pinned formula sheet): useful
   339	  to keep the neural parts honest and hardware-independent, REPORTED always,
   340	  BINDING never — no "compute-matched" claim may cite it alone [STIPULATED:
   341	  ASM-0810; corrects technical error #7].
   342	
   343	Precedent that the symbolic side can be genuinely cheap on the measured vector —
   344	and that the accounting has to be measured, not assumed:
   345	
   346	- PREMISE: the deterministic engine answers covered queries at 5.29–7.82 µs/query,
   347	  and the f2b system-level cost ratio vs the 1.7B host was measured at 0.103
   348	  [MEASURED: registry/verdicts/l3a.json + registry/verdicts/a5.json engine timing;
   349	  registry/verdicts/f2b-replicate.json cost_ratio_vs_R3].

   351	### 1.3a The lifecycle ledger — KOT-LIFE/1
   352	
   353	The comparison this programme runs is a *deployment-efficiency* comparison, not an
   354	equal-total-investment comparison — legitimate, but it must be named accurately
   355	and priced [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, answering review "add a lifecycle ledger"].
   356	
   357	- DECISION: every W1-relevant system (S and every comparator) publishes a
   358	  **lifecycle ledger**: (1) donor pretraining provenance and compute where known
   359	  (dimension-drop arms *inherit* their donor's training compute — stated, never
   360	  netted out); (2) architecture fine-tuning + hyperparameter-search compute
   361	  actually spent (the tuning-symmetry audit trail, §2.3); (3) store costs:
   362	  authoring, parsing, embedding, indexing, and human review, in hours and $;
   363	  (4) total cost of ownership amortised at three pinned deployment volumes —
   364	  10^3, 10^6, 10^9 queries — using the measured KOT-COST/2 energy/latency figures.
   365	  Training longer to obtain a smaller model is a real deployment strategy and
   366	  optimal size depends on inference volume (REVIEW-CITED: Sardana et al., "Beyond
   367	  Chinchilla-Optimal"; verified by P3-LR-SYS). The ledger CO-REPORTS with every W1
   368	  claim and feeds kill condition K-P3v2(6); it does not gate W1 numerically
   369	  because store-authoring hours and GPU-hours have no non-arbitrary common unit —
   370	  the honesty mechanism is mandatory publication + the kill condition, not a
   371	  fudged exchange rate [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   372	
   373	### 1.4 The exact win condition and the exact shrink objective
   374	
   375	The revision-1 ±10% band construction is WITHDRAWN as gameable (padding into a
   376	favourable band; dodging a strong model just outside it; empty band
   377	intersections; losing to a smaller/cheaper dominator while "winning the
   378	neighbourhood") [STIPULATED: ASM-0810, accepting review ranked concern #1 in
   379	full].
   380	
   381	**W1 (the resource-frontier competitive claim), verbatim:** at rung R-k, with a
   382	pre-registered resource budget vector
   383	`B_k = (max canonically-packed deployment bytes; max p95 latency; max energy/query;
   384	max peak accelerator memory + host RSS)`, a Programme-3 system S beats the
   385	baseline frontier iff, under the frozen KOT-AI-INDEX/2 version for that rung,
   386	own-harness:
   387	
   388	1. **Admissibility:** S fits within EVERY component of B_k, measured under
   389	   KOT-SIZE/2 + KOT-COST/2, warm and cold conditions per the P3-D-HW protocol
   390	   (energy explicitly binds here — fixing revision 1's omission of energy from
   391	   W1 while KOT-COST claimed it); and
   392	2. **Frontier dominance:** for EVERY comparator C in the pre-registered comparator
   393	   set F(B_k) — every pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline
   394	   family that fits within every component of B_k, explicitly INCLUDING systems
   395	   smaller or cheaper than S, each optimised for the budget by the frontier-builder
   396	   (§2.3) — the simultaneous one-sided 95% lower confidence bound satisfies
   397	   `LCB95(INDEX(S) − INDEX(C)) > δ_k`, with family-wise error control across the
   398	   comparator set and a hierarchical bootstrap over benchmark families and items
   399	   preserving paired predictions (§2.5); and
   400	3. the decontamination audit (§2.2) AND the sealed-evaluation consistency gate
   401	   (§2.2a) pass; and
   402	4. the factorial controls (§2.2) behave as pre-registered (derangement/permutation
   403	   destroys any store-content-attributed component; the aligned-non-kernel and
   404	   matched-generic-RAG results are REPORTED as the attribution split, whatever
   405	   they show); and
   406	5. the lifecycle ledger (§1.3a) is published alongside.
   407	
   408	Reporting: the full **staircase/Pareto envelope** of neural and neural-retrieval
   409	points across budgets (index vs bytes, index vs cost) is published with S plotted
   410	against it — W1 means S lies significantly above the envelope at its budget, not
   411	that it won a favourable neighbourhood.
   412	
   413	**Scope of the claim licensed by a W1 PASS, verbatim:** "S exceeds every
   414	pre-registered, reproducible open comparator and baseline family searched under
   415	budget B_k by ≥ δ_k on KOT-AI-INDEX/2-vN" — NEVER "higher than all other models of
   416	the same size and compute"; the literal universal is untestable and is hereby
   417	retired from every claim surface [STIPULATED: ASM-0810].
   418	
   419	**W2 (the shrink objective), verbatim:** once W1 first holds at any rung, minimise
   420	KOT-SIZE/2 figure-(1) bytes subject to `INDEX(S) ≥ INDEX(M_ref)` where M_ref is a
   421	named, pinned open reference model (initial M_ref = SmolLM2-1.7B own-harness;
   422	revisited by maintainer fork per index version). The deliverable is the measured
   423	**Pareto frontier over (bytes, resource vector, index)**; headline success = at
   424	least one point that strictly dominates every measurable open point at the small
   425	end. Consistency rule (fixing the review's W2 finding): the R-2 sweep inside
   426	P3-E-SCALE-2 runs ONLY conditional on W1 passing at R-2; any compression curve
   427	produced before a W1 PASS (e.g. H-DD dose-response curves) is labelled
   428	**DIAGNOSTIC**, never W2 [STIPULATED: ASM-0817].
   429	
   430	Falsifiability: W1 is falsified per-rung by failing its own margin against the
   431	frontier; the programme has quantitative kill conditions in §7. There is no
   432	"moral victory" clause: frontier parity is a FAIL of W1, however interesting the
   433	architecture.

   437	## 2. The measurement + fair-comparison framework (where the programme lives or dies)
   438	
   439	Comparing a neurosymbolic system against the neural/RAG frontier at a fixed
   440	resource budget is the single hardest methodological problem here — harder than
   441	any individual architecture. The framework is frozen as **P3-MF (KOT-FAIR/2)**
   442	before any Phase-2 W1-relevant experiment freezes, and it now begins with an
   443	explicit threat model.
   444	
   445	### 2.0 The threat model (new; P3-D-THREAT)
   446	
   447	- DECISION: KOT-FAIR/2 includes a formal **resource/baseline threat model**: an
   448	  enumerated adversary analysis of how each metric can be gamed by either side —
   449	  packaging games (padding, base-image migration, remote dependencies), band/
   450	  frontier games (comparator dodging, budget shopping), cost games (warm-cache-only
   451	  reporting, output-length inflation/deflation, batch-size shopping,
   452	  symbolic-work-hiding), index games (suite shopping, abstention gaming, domain
   453	  reweighting), store games (answer-key encoding, error-analysis-driven authoring,
   454	  researcher adaptation to the frozen suite), and tuning games (unequal search
   455	  compute behind equal config counts) — each with its named mechanised counter in
   456	  this section. The threat model is a frozen artifact; a game not counterable at
   457	  freeze time is documented as a standing limitation on every claim it touches
   458	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812; answers review §4 "formal resource/baseline threat
   459	  model"].
   460	
   461	### 2.1 Confound A — size/packaging asymmetry
   462	
   463	Answered by KOT-SIZE/2 (§1.2): canonical packing script, six co-reported figures,
   464	frozen base image, remote-dependency rule, artifact-freeze (store hash pinned at
   465	eval time, so "grow the store after measuring" is impossible by construction), and
   466	peak-RSS/cold-start measurement so lazily-paged stores pay their true cost on the
   467	resource vector.
   468	
   469	### 2.2 Confound B — the answer-key problem (the deepest one)
   470	
   471	A store can encode benchmark answers far more directly than weights can. The f2b
   472	lesson is exactly this in miniature:
   473	
   474	- PREMISE: the f2b verifier accepts iff the answer string-equals the canonical
   475	  record while gold is DEFINED by that same equality, so the measured +0.1507 is
   476	  provably consistent with "aligned answer key + retry", and the shuffled control
   477	  cannot discriminate content from alignment [MEASURED:
   478	  registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json does_not_license].
   479	
   480	Mechanised answers, all mandatory for any W1 claim [STIPULATED: ASM-0812]:
   481	
   482	1. **Decontamination audit (hard gate, win-voiding):** automated n-gram overlap +
   483	   embedding-similarity screen of every kernel/world/store record against every
   484	   item of the frozen index suite, pinned threshold, human spot-check protocol on
   485	   flagged pairs; stores may not be authored, selected, or grown using any index
   100	  and SYS.md §2's admissibility rule as a single binding rule; answers the
   101	  review's energy contradiction]: energy is always REPORTED per arm with its
   102	  declared measurement boundary, or MISSING where no counter exists (never
   103	  modeled from TDP or utilisation); it is a BINDING admissibility component of
   104	  B_k only where every compared arm carries a measurement at the SAME declared
   105	  boundary, same instrument class, same rig; a MISSING or boundary-mismatched
   106	  cell excludes the energy dimension from that comparison (disclosed in the
   107	  consuming prereg and verdict) and B_k binds on the remaining components. On the
   108	  pool as currently surveyed, CPU-side RAPL is absent on the local box and energy
   109	  cells are GPU-NVML-boundary at best [LIT-BACKED: SYS.md §2–§3, measured-locally
   110	  section, 2026-07-10];
   111	- `mem_max` = (peak accelerator memory, host RSS) pair.
   112	
   113	All four components are measured by the P3-D-HW rig; the builder consumes that rig,
   114	it does not define it [STIPULATED: ASM-0850].
   115	
   116	### 1.2 The budget derivation rule (the anti-budget-shopping counter)
   117	
   118	- DECISION: **one primary budget vector per rung**, derived mechanically from the
   119	  rung's named anchor model and pinned at RUNG ENTRY, before any candidate system
   120	  S is measured at that rung: `B_k := the anchor's own measured resource vector`
   121	  — bytes = the anchor's canonically-packed bf16 bytes; latency/energy/memory =
   122	  the anchor's measured figures on the pinned rig under the pinned index-suite
   123	  workload, at multiplier ×1.0 on every component [STIPULATED: ASM-0810
   124	  operationalised; ASM-0850]. Rung
   125	  anchors are the programme's existing orientation anchors (R-1 = SmolLM2-135M,
   126	  R-2 = SmolLM2-360M, R-3 = SmolLM2-1.7B; programme §4), pinned as name+revision
   127	  by P3-D-BASE.
   128	- DECISION: additional budget vectors at a rung (e.g. a tighter-bytes point for
   129	  W2 shrink work) may be registered ONLY before any S candidate is measured at
   130	  that rung; after first S measurement, the budget set at that rung is immutable
   131	  for that index version. If S does not fit B_k, S fails ADMISSIBILITY — there is
   132	  no post-hoc budget inflation, and no "S defines its own budget" path
   133	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0810/ASM-0812 operationalised; ASM-0850; counters the
   134	  threat-model "budget shopping" channel, programme §2.0].
   135	- Rationale (why anchor-derived, not S-derived): deriving B_k from S's measured
   136	  resources would let S's packaging choices move the goalposts — the review's
   137	  band-gaming concern in new clothes. Anchor-derived budgets are computable before
   138	  any architecture exists and are exactly what "comparably-resourced" means to an
   139	  outside reader [STIPULATED: ASM-0850].
   140	
   141	### 1.3 F(B_k) and the envelope — formal semantics
   142	
   143	- **F(B_k)** = the set of comparator systems C such that (i) C is built by this
   144	  builder from the pinned search space (§2–§4) or nominated per §7.2, (ii) C is
   145	  ADMISSIBLE (fits every component of B_k, warm and cold, measured under
   350	  line); ties broken by lower measured cost vector (lexicographic: energy where
   351	  the §1.1 rule makes it comparable, then p95). Near-floor MC components are
   352	  excluded from the SELECTION metric because their signal is noise near chance
   353	  [LIT-BACKED: EVAL.md §5 (Schaeffer arXiv:2304.15004, 2023)] — they remain in
   354	  the frozen-suite MEASUREMENT of the final comparators [STIPULATED: ASM-0812
   355	  fixed-selection-rule clause executed; ASM-0851].
   356	
   357	### 3.3 Total-tuning-compute symmetry (the T_k meter, dimensionally closed)
   358	
   359	- DECISION: tuning symmetry binds on **total tuning compute actually spent**,
   360	  metered as a **RESOURCE VECTOR with separately capped components** — `T_k =
   361	  (accelerator-hours, CPU-core-hours)`, each logged per arm into KOT-LIFE/1 —
   362	  NOT config counts (ASM-0812; data-matched ≠ compute-matched [LIT-BACKED:
   363	  EVAL.md §7 (BabyLM-2 findings arXiv:2412.05149, 2024)]), and NOT a GPU-hour
   364	  scalar (revision 1 capped GPU-hours only, leaving CPU-heavy indexing/search
   365	  logged but uncapped — the review's dimensional-closure defect, corrected)
   366	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0812 tuning-symmetry operationalised; ASM-0852].
   367	- DECISION: the unit of "an arm" is **mechanical, not judgemental** [STIPULATED:
   368	  ASM-0852; answers the review's family-multiplication defect]: an arm is one row
   369	  of the **arm table** — a closed, enumerated list of ledger keys registered at
   370	  rung entry alongside B_k. R-1 comparator keys: {C1-quant, C2-prune, C2-distil,
   371	  C3-RAG-matched, C3-RAG-free, C4-TTC} (six; no C5 key at R-1 — H-GU is an R-0
   372	  family; a C5-twin key exists only at rungs where a from-scratch S is live),
   373	  plus one key for S. Every tuning/search/recovery/verifier-training job carries
   374	  exactly one key before launch; a job not attributable to a key is billed to
   375	  the largest-spend arm (fail-closed); **adding a row to the arm table after rung
   376	  entry is a frontier VERSION bump** — family boundaries cannot silently multiply
   377	  allowance. Each arm (and S) spends ≤ T_k per component; total comparator tuning
   378	  is bounded by |arm table| × T_k by construction; the meter is the builder's own
   379	  job-level accounting, audit-trailed. Proposed values, maintainer-adjustable at
   380	  KOT-FAIR/2 freeze and reconciled against §10's rung totals: **T_1 = (4 GPU-h,
   381	  40 CPU-core-h)/arm; T_2 = (20 GPU-h, 200 CPU-core-h)/arm; T_3 = (80 GPU-h,
   382	  600 CPU-core-h)/arm**; R-0 arms run inside the free pool with the same logging
   383	  [STIPULATED: ASM-0852]. An S that spends more than T_k has not won (ASM-0812
   384	  verbatim); a comparator arm that exhausts T_k freezes at its best logged config.
   385	- Donor pretraining compute is INHERITED and declared on the lifecycle ledger
   386	  (KOT-LIFE/1), never netted out and never counted against T_k — symmetric, since
   387	  S's families also start from donors [STIPULATED: ASM-0852].

   391	## 4. The adaptive test-time-compute recipe (resolving RAG.md's five OPEN items)
   392	
   393	The lit-review verdict this section answers: "'implement Snell-style' is not yet
   394	an executable baseline recipe" [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §4/§7.6/§8]. The verified
   395	envelope it must respect: the >4× and 14× findings are separate, conditional,
   468	## 5. The matched-retrieval rules (resolving RAG.md §6's five OPEN rules)
   469	
   470	These five rules had "no published precedent" and were left open by the lit
   471	review [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §7.1]. They are resolved here as **binding defaults**
   472	because the frontier's own matched-corpus arm (§2.3a) cannot be built without
   473	them; **P3-D-RAGC inherits them verbatim and instantiates the factorial cells on
   474	top; it may refine a rule only by registered amendment, never silently**
   475	[STIPULATED: ASM-0853].
   476	
   477	**Rule 1 — source-content derivation + information-parity criterion.** One pinned
   478	source snapshot (content hash) per vertical/corpus. Each arm's indexed objects are
   479	derived by a **pinned, automated, seed-pinned derivation script** (kernel records
   480	via the standing mint pipeline version; passages via a pinned chunker
   481	(size/overlap pinned); triples via a pinned extractor; tool schemas via a pinned
   482	generator) — human curation inside a derivation is permitted only if its hours are
   483	logged to KOT-LIFE/1 and disclosed. Parity is enforced as four MEASURED checks,
   484	and no further: (a) **source parity** — every arm's derivation consumes the
   485	identical snapshot, no arm-specific extra sources; (b) **byte cap parity** — every
   486	arm's packed indexed-object bytes ≤ the same store-byte allocation within B_k
   487	(equal cap, not forced-equal usage); (c) **screen parity** — the §2.2
   488	decontamination screen applies to every arm's derived objects identically;
   489	(d) **effort disclosure** — authoring/curation/build compute per arm co-reported
   490	(no fudged exchange rate, consistent with KOT-LIFE/1's no-common-unit rule).
   491	Residual honesty line, carried verbatim on every consuming claim: *information
   492	parity beyond these four checks cannot be certified — converting one snapshot
   493	into different object types changes the retrieval problem itself; this is a
   494	standing limitation, not a solved problem* [STIPULATED: ASM-0853; the RAG.md §6.1
   495	scope finding adopted].
   496	
   497	**Rule 2 — the shared/matched cell vs the native cell (CORRECTED per the
   498	2026-07-11 external review, ranked concern 1).** Two mandatory cells, **BOTH
   499	members of F(B_k) whenever S uses a store**, each with a distinct claim licence
   500	[STIPULATED: ASM-0853; ASM-0812(iii) executed verbatim]:
   501	
   502	- the **SHARED/MATCHED cell** — one pinned retriever+index (the §2.3a cells:
   503	  BM25 + one pinned small dense embedder) over the SAME pinned corpus snapshot
   504	  at the SAME budgets, run over a common text serialisation of every arm's
   505	  objects — **is the programme's stipulated §2.3(iii) control** ("SAME corpus,
   506	  SAME retriever/index, SAME budgets as the kernel arm") and is a **REQUIRED
   507	  member of the W1 control set AND of F(B_k)**: a W1 claim that has not beaten
   508	  this cell is void. Claim licence: content-representation attribution at fixed
   509	  retrieval, plus frontier membership. Its structural handicap for arms whose
   510	  native retrieval is not dense-over-text is DISCLOSED beside its result — it is
   511	  never grounds for excluding the cell (revision 1 used exactly that argument to
   512	  demote this cell to an instrument, contradicting the binding KOT-FAIR/2
   513	  contract; corrected).
   514	- the **NATIVE cell** — each arm retrieves the way its architecture natively
   515	  does (kernel addressing for S; the RAG arm's best in-budget retriever) under
   516	  the same resource budgets — **ADDITIONALLY enters F(B_k)** as the
   517	  strongest-system frontier point. Claim licence: "the best each architecture
   518	  can do at the budget". It supplements the shared/matched cell; it never
   519	  replaces it.
   520	
   521	Every consuming prereg names which cell licenses which sentence.
   522	
   523	**Rule 3 — retrieved-token/context budgets + ordering parity.** Pinned per rung:
   524	max retrieved tokens per query (identical across arms), max context length,
   525	context assembly order = retrieval-score-descending (no arm may hand-place gold
   526	material at context edges — the position confound), and a position-shuffle
   527	control cell lives in RAGC's factorial [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §6.4,
   528	Lost-in-the-Middle; STIPULATED: ASM-0853].
   529	
   530	**Rule 4 — generator / executor / retry / tuning parity.** Where the comparison
   531	is store-vs-store (matched-corpus arm vs S), the generator checkpoint, decoding
   532	parameters, retry budget (same max-k), abstention accounting, and tool-executor
   533	semantics are IDENTICAL across arms; tuning ≤ T_k per arm (§3.3). Where the
   534	comparison is whole-system frontier (free-corpus arm), the generator may differ —
   535	that arm's licence is "a better baseline exists at this budget", not attribution
   536	[STIPULATED: ASM-0853].
   537	
   538	**Rule 5 — construction leakage + authoring effort.** Every construction pass
   539	(embedding sweeps, LLM extraction, selector training — the TinyAgent DeBERTa
   540	lesson [LIT-BACKED: RAG.md §1]) is booked as build compute in KOT-LIFE/1 and its
   541	artifacts' bytes in KOT-SIZE/2 figures (1)/(5) on EVERY arm identically.
   542	Order-of-operations rule: derivation/construction pipelines are frozen
   543	(content-hashed) BEFORE any frozen-suite item is drawn; the §2.2 decontamination
   544	screen applies to construction INPUTS as well as outputs; a store or index whose
   545	construction post-dates suite exposure is VOID for W1 use [STIPULATED: ASM-0853].
   680	its own F-CAL gates (A4 by construction can fail against outside nominations);
   681	the frontier it produces is falsified per rung by W1's own test; and the design's
   682	central stipulation — that a best-effort-optimised, honestly-ledgered baseline
   683	frontier at 100M–2B is CONSTRUCTIBLE inside the available compute (free pool
   684	through R-2; a secured allocation at R-3, §10) — is falsified if the §10 budget
   685	is exhausted before A1–A4 pass [STIPULATED: ASM-0854].

   689	## 9. What the frontier-builder consumes and emits (interface table)
   690	
   691	| Interface | Direction | Contract |
   692	|---|---|---|
   693	| P3-D-INDEX (KOT-AI-INDEX/2, dev split, analysis plan) | consumes | dev split + floor/exclusion criteria + FWER procedure + comparator-covariance estimation + per-component class map (§4.2); builder never defines index content |

item.text:
## Verdict

Strong baseline concept, but not yet sound to commit as the experiment-driving Phase-1 design. The implementation is broadly feasible at 135M–360M on modest corpora, and the NL-boundary discipline is mostly correct. However, the document currently overclaims causal attribution, has an invalid calibration gate, and does not close KOT-FAIR/2 admissibility/accounting.

I would revise before creating `P3-T-RAGC-IMPL` or `P3-E-RAGC-CAL`. Committing it only as an explicitly provisional draft would be defensible.

## Assessment

1. Concrete/buildable at 100M–2B: partially yes.

The chunker, BM25/dense indexes, SQLite/Datalog executor, perturbations, retry wrapper, and manifest are ordinary engineering at these model sizes. The R-1 implementation is realistic.

But the executable scope is not closed:

- The design alternates between a “closed full arm set” and only “3–5 configs” in its cost estimate. The actual matrix—arms × BM25/dense × perturbations × acceptance signals × rungs—can be materially larger than the arithmetic at [RAGC.md:531](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:531).
- GR-C does not pin the query language, expressivity envelope, recursive-query semantics, tool-call protocol, or mapping from kernel operations to SQL/Datalog operations. “SQLite or Datalog” is still a design fork, not an executable specification [RAGC.md:344](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:344).
- GR-D leaves the acceptance mechanism to each consuming experiment. A learned verifier also needs training data construction, split discipline, calibration, and supervision-leakage rules [RAGC.md:322](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:322).
- “FlashRAG-class” is not an implementation pin. The actual harness, release, supported retrievers, and compatibility with 135M hosts remain unresolved [RAGC.md:269](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:269).

So: implementable architecture, incomplete executable design.

2. Cheap, decisive, falsifiable, NL-wall honest: NL-honest, but not yet decisive.

The good part is important: natural text remains native for generic RAG, while any natural-input kernel arm must pay the NLB cost. OR and P6 are explicitly diagnostic and barred from W1. That correctly respects the measured 47.6%/41.6% NL failures and does not smuggle an oracle into a deployment claim.

The worked a5/f2b experiment is nevertheless formal-input/G2 evidence only. It can decisively de-confound the existing `+0.1507` formal-slice result, but it cannot support real-input usefulness until the kernel side passes NLB. That limitation should be stated directly in §8.3, not merely inherited from the programme.

More seriously, RAGC-CAL is instrument validation, not a cheap decisive test of the baseline’s central scientific claim. There is no frozen primary contrast, effect margin, kill rule, power target, or maximum spend for the proposed f2b de-confound. Those are delegated elsewhere. “Envelope exhausted before V1–V3 pass” tests engineering completion, not whether generic RAG/tool use explains the observed gain.

3. Technical assumptions/gaps likely to fail: material issues remain.

The central six-way factorisation is not actually achieved by the listed pairwise contrasts:

- `S − GR-A` changes derived representation, index contents, evidence rendering, interface, and often execution—not merely “kernel vs conventional retrieval.”
- `S − GR-C` changes engine, query library, tool schema, planner/API surface, and possibly expressivity. It does not isolate the engine.
- `S − GR-D` changes verifier information, error profile, calibration, and acceptance rate. Matching the loop shape does not isolate “kernel verifier content.”
- No contrast isolates the sixth factor, integration, or estimates interactions.

These are useful system comparisons, but not factorial causal identification. The claims at [RAGC.md:404](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:404) and the K-P3v2(4) wiring are stronger than the cells license.

4. KOT-FAIR/2 and accounting: correctly oriented, incompletely wired.

The design correctly includes source pins, index/build cost, human effort, tuning compute, retrieved-token parity, construction leakage, and lifecycle reporting.

The missing piece is total-system admissibility. `store_bytes(B_k)` is never derived from the rung’s total deployment budget. Since `B_k` is anchor-derived, an anchor-sized generator plus any nonzero store/index cannot fit the anchor’s canonical-byte ceiling. The design needs an explicit residual allocation:

`store_bytes ≤ B_k.bytes − generator − retriever − verifier − executor − other artifacts`

or a smaller in-budget generator. Equal store caps alone do not establish KOT-FAIR/2 admissibility.

The manifest also lacks references to the complete KOT-SIZE/2 six-figure report and measured KOT-COST/2 vector—p95, energy, RSS/accelerator memory, I/O, warm/cold behavior—for GR-C and GR-D [RAGC.md:443](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:443). Build-ledger rows are necessary but insufficient.

## Technical errors

- “Same retriever/index” over different object sets is technically impossible. The comparison can share retriever algorithm, weights, configuration, and build procedure; each representation necessarily produces a different index.
- V2’s expected ordering `OR ≥ GR-A ≥ R0` is not a valid instrument invariant. Relevant retrieval can hurt generation, and oracle delivery can be worse than retrieved context because of representation or context effects. Only a preregistered discrimination contrast with uncertainty is defensible [RAGC.md:507](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:507).
- V2 calls itself LCB-based but specifies an LCB only for `OR − R0`, not for both stated inequalities.
- The ε-parity rule uses observed `|κ_S−κ_GR| ≤ ε` without uncertainty. Parity needs a paired equivalence/non-inferiority CI; failure to prove equivalence should downgrade attribution.
- κ measures provenance-span survival, not semantic correctness. An incorrectly extracted triple pointing at the right span counts as covered. The round-1 requirement for source-class precision auditing is missing.
- P6 is named “answerable fraction” but measures generator task accuracy under oracle delivery. It conflates derivation correctness, representation rendering, and generator consumption.
- “Byte-identical dense indexes within a band” is contradictory and usually unrealistic for multithreaded/ANN builds. Pin deterministic embeddings/logical records and test retrieval equivalence or a specified index tolerance [RAGC.md:512](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:512).
- “Tool-execution success” is generally not an answer-validity signal; it can accept a syntactically valid but semantically irrelevant call.
- The statistics reference to “§2.5” is broken/ambiguous; RAGC has no §2.5 [RAGC.md:432](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:432).
- GR-A is called “best generic system built on the evidence” after parity failure, but that licence belongs to GR-B/native search, not necessarily the shared-lens GR-A [RAGC.md:196](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/design/RAGC.md:196).

## Three most important concerns, ranked

1. **The claimed six-factor causal attribution is invalid.** The proposed contrasts change multiple factors and do not measure interactions or integration.
2. **KOT-FAIR/2 admissibility is not closed.** Store caps are not derived from total `B_k`, and GR-C/GR-D lack complete KOT-SIZE/2 and KOT-COST/2 measurement contracts.
3. **The “cheap decisive test” is not preregistration-ready.** V2 is statistically/causally invalid, κ parity lacks uncertainty and precision auditing, and the f2b de-confound has no primary margin, kill rule, power, or spend stop.

**Recommendation: do not drive implementation/experiment beads from this revision.** Repair those three items first. After repair, the design is a strong and necessary Programme-3 control, and the a5/f2b R-1 formal-slice de-confound is the right first cheap experiment—explicitly G2/formal-only until NLB clears.
